heading: Clean electricity production credit
label: 45Y Clean electricity production credit

children.label: i
children.text: produced by the taxpayer at a qualified facility, and

children.label: I
children.text: sold by the taxpayer to an unrelated person during the taxable year, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a qualified facility which is equipped with a metering device which is owned and operated by an unrelated person, sold, consumed, or stored by the taxpayer during the taxable year, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the applicable amount with respect to such qualified facility.

children.heading: Base amount
children.label: A Base amount
children.text: Subject to subsection (g)(7), in the case of any qualified facility which is not described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (B) and does not satisfy the requirements described in clause (iii) of such subparagraph, the applicable amount shall be 0.3 cents.

children.label: i
children.text: with a maximum net output of less than 1 megawatt (as measured in alternating current),

children.label: ii
children.text: the construction of which begins prior to the date that is 60 days after the Secretary publishes guidance with respect to the requirements of paragraphs (9) and (10) of subsection (g), or

children.label: I
children.text: satisfies the requirements under paragraph (9) of subsection (g), and

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to the construction of such facility, satisfies the requirements under paragraph (10) of subsection (g),

children.label: i
children.text: which is used for the generation of electricity,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is placed in service after , andDecember 31, 2024

children.label: iii
children.text: for which the greenhouse gas emissions rate (as determined under paragraph (2)) is not greater than zero.

children.heading: 10-year production credit
children.label: B 10-year production credit
children.text: For purposes of this section, a facility shall only be treated as a qualified facility during the 10-year period beginning on the date the facility was originally placed in service.

children.heading: Expansion of facility; incremental production
children.label: C Expansion of facility; incremental production
i
children.text: A new unit which is placed in service after .December 31, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: Any additions of capacity which are placed in service after .December 31, 2024

children.label: i
children.text: determinations by, or reports to, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (including interconnection agreements), the Nuclear Regulatory Commission, or any similar entity, reflecting additions of capacity,

children.label: ii
children.text: determinations or reports reflecting additions of capacity made by an independent professional engineer,

children.label: iii
children.text: reports to, or issued by, regional transmission organizations or independent system operators reflecting additions of capacity, or

children.label: iv
children.text: any other method or manner provided by the Secretary.

children.heading: Coordination with other credits1So in original. There are two subpars. (E).1
children.label: E Coordination with other credits1So in original. There are two subpars. (E).1
children.text: The term “qualified facility” shall not include any facility for which a credit determined under section 45, 45J, 45Q, 45U, 48, 48A, or 48E is allowed under section 38 for the taxable year or any prior taxable year.

children.heading: Material assistance from prohibited foreign entities1
children.label: E Material assistance from prohibited foreign entities1
children.text: The term “qualified facility” shall not include any facility for which construction begins after , if the construction of such facility includes any material assistance from a prohibited foreign entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(52)).December 31, 2025

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “greenhouse gas emissions rate” means the amount of greenhouse gases emitted into the atmosphere by a facility in the production of electricity, expressed as grams of COe per KWh.2

children.heading: Fuel combustion and gasification
children.label: B Fuel combustion and gasification
children.text: In the case of a facility which produces electricity through combustion or gasification, the greenhouse gas emissions rate for such facility shall be equal to the net rate of greenhouse gases emitted into the atmosphere by such facility (taking into account lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions, as described in section 211()(1)(H) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)(H))) in the production of electricity, expressed as grams of COe per KWh.oo42 U.S.C. 75452

children.heading: Establishment of emissions rates for facilities
children.label: C Establishment of emissions rates for facilities

children.heading: Publishing emissions rates
children.label: i Publishing emissions rates
children.text: The Secretary shall annually publish a table that sets forth the greenhouse gas emissions rates for types or categories of facilities, which a taxpayer shall use for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Provisional emissions rate
children.label: ii Provisional emissions rate
children.text: In the case of any facility for which an emissions rate has not been established by the Secretary, a taxpayer which owns such facility may file a petition with the Secretary for determination of the emissions rate with respect to such facility.

children.heading: Existing studies
children.label: iii Existing studies
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), in determining greenhouse gas emissions rates for types or categories of facilities for the purpose of determining whether a facility satisfies the requirements under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consider studies published on or before the date of enactment of this clause which demonstrate a net lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions rate which is not greater than zero using widely accepted lifecycle assessment concepts, such as concepts described in standards developed by the International Organization for Standardization.

children.heading: Carbon capture and sequestration equipment
children.label: D Carbon capture and sequestration equipment
i
children.text: pursuant to any regulations established under paragraph (2) of section 45Q(f), disposed of by the taxpayer in secure geological storage, or

children.label: ii
children.text: utilized by the taxpayer in a manner described in paragraph (5) of such section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a calendar year beginning after 2024, the 0.3 cent amount in paragraph (2)(A) of subsection (a) and the 1.5 cent amount in paragraph (2)(B) of such subsection shall each be adjusted by multiplying such amount by the inflation adjustment factor for the calendar year in which the sale, consumption, or storage of the electricity occurs. If the 0.3 cent amount as increased under this paragraph is not a multiple of 0.05 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.05 cent. If the 1.5 cent amount as increased under this paragraph is not a multiple of 0.1 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.1 cent.

children.heading: Annual computation
children.label: 2 Annual computation
children.text: The Secretary shall, not later than April 1 of each calendar year, determine and publish in the Federal Register the inflation adjustment factor for such calendar year in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment factor
children.label: 3 Inflation adjustment factor
children.text: The term “inflation adjustment factor” means, with respect to a calendar year, a fraction the numerator of which is the GDP implicit price deflator for the preceding calendar year and the denominator of which is the GDP implicit price deflator for the calendar year 1992. The term “GDP implicit price deflator” means the most recent revision of the implicit price deflator for the gross domestic product as computed and published by the Department of Commerce before March 15 of the calendar year.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the credit determined under subsection (a) without regard to this subsection, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the phase-out percentage under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: for a facility the construction of which begins during the first calendar year following the applicable year, 100 percent,

children.label: B
children.text: for a facility the construction of which begins during the second calendar year following the applicable year, 75 percent,

children.label: C
children.text: for a facility the construction of which begins during the third calendar year following the applicable year, 50 percent, and

children.label: D
children.text: for a facility the construction of which begins during any calendar year subsequent to the calendar year described in subparagraph (C), 0 percent.

children.heading: Applicable year
children.label: 3 Applicable year
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable year” means calendar year 2032.

children.heading: Termination for wind and solar facilities
children.label: 4 Termination for wind and solar facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This section shall not apply with respect to any applicable facility placed in service after .December 31, 2027

children.label: i
children.text: uses wind to produce electricity (within the meaning of such term as used in section 45(d)(1), as determined without regard to any requirement under such section with respect to the date on which construction of property begins), or

children.label: ii
children.text: uses solar energy to produce electricity (within the meaning of such term as used in section 45(d)(4), as determined without regard to any requirement under such section with respect to the date on which construction of property begins).

children.heading: COe per KWh2
children.label: 1 COe per KWh2
children.text: The term “COe per KWh” means, with respect to any greenhouse gas, the equivalent carbon dioxide (as determined based on global warming potential) per kilowatt hour of electricity produced.2

children.heading: Greenhouse gas
children.label: 2 Greenhouse gas
children.text: The term “greenhouse gas” has the same meaning given such term under section 211()(1)(G) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)(G)), as in effect on the date of the enactment of this section.oo42 U.S.C. 7545

children.label: A
children.text: would otherwise be released into the atmosphere as industrial emission of greenhouse gas,

children.label: B
children.text: is measured at the source of capture and verified at the point of disposal or utilization, and

children.label: C
children.text: is captured and disposed or utilized within the United States (within the meaning of section 638(1)) or a possession of the United States (within the meaning of section 638(2)).

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: f Guidance
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall issue guidance regarding implementation of this section, including calculation of greenhouse gas emission rates for qualified facilities and determination of clean electricity production credits under this section.January 1, 2025

children.heading: Only production in the United States taken into account
children.label: 1 Only production in the United States taken into account
A
children.text: the United States (within the meaning of section 638(1)), or

children.label: B
children.text: a possession of the United States (within the meaning of section 638(2)).

children.heading: Combined heat and power system property
children.label: 2 Combined heat and power system property
i
children.text: the kilowatt hours of electricity produced by a taxpayer at a qualified facility shall include any production in the form of useful thermal energy by any combined heat and power system property within such facility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of greenhouse gases emitted into the atmosphere by such facility in the production of such useful thermal energy shall be included for purposes of determining the greenhouse gas emissions rate for such facility.

children.heading: Combined heat and power system property
children.label: B Combined heat and power system property
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “combined heat and power system property” has the same meaning given such term by section 48(c)(3) (without regard to subparagraphs (A)(iv), (B), and (D) thereof).

children.heading: Conversion from BTU to KWh
children.label: C Conversion from BTU to KWh
I
children.text: the total useful thermal energy produced by the combined heat and power system property within the qualified facility, divided by

children.label: II
children.text: the heat rate for such facility.

children.heading: Heat rate
children.label: ii Heat rate
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “heat rate” means the amount of energy used by the qualified facility to generate 1 kilowatt hour of electricity, expressed as British thermal units per net kilowatt hour generated.

children.heading: Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.label: 3 Production attributable to the taxpayer
children.text: In the case of a qualified facility in which more than 1 person has an ownership interest, except to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, production from the facility shall be allocated among such persons in proportion to their respective ownership interests in the gross sales from such facility.

children.heading: Related persons
children.label: 4 Related persons
children.text: Persons shall be treated as related to each other if such persons would be treated as a single employer under the regulations prescribed under section 52(b). In the case of a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group of corporations filing a consolidated return, such corporation shall be treated as selling electricity to an unrelated person if such electricity is sold to such a person by another member of such group.

children.heading: Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.label: 5 Pass-thru in the case of estates and trusts
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, rules similar to the rules of subsection (d) of section 52 shall apply.

children.heading: Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative
children.label: 6 Allocation of credit to patrons of agricultural cooperative

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In the case of an eligible cooperative organization, any portion of the credit determined under subsection (a) for the taxable year may, at the election of the organization, be apportioned among patrons of the organization on the basis of the amount of business done by the patrons during the taxable year.

children.heading: Form and effect of election
children.label: ii Form and effect of election
children.text: An election under clause (i) for any taxable year shall be made on a timely filed return for such year. Such election, once made, shall be irrevocable for such taxable year. Such election shall not take effect unless the organization designates the apportionment as such in a written notice mailed to its patrons during the payment period described in section 1382(d).

children.heading: Treatment of organizations and patrons
children.label: B Treatment of organizations and patrons
i
children.text: shall not be included in the amount determined under subsection (a) with respect to the organization for the taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be included in the amount determined under subsection (a) for the first taxable year of each patron ending on or after the last day of the payment period (as defined in section 1382(d)) for the taxable year of the organization or, if earlier, for the taxable year of each patron ending on or after the date on which the patron receives notice from the cooperative of the apportionment.

children.heading: Special rules for decrease in credits for taxable year
children.label: C Special rules for decrease in credits for taxable year
i
children.text: such reduction, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount not apportioned to such patrons under subparagraph (A) for the taxable year,

children.heading: Eligible cooperative defined
children.label: D Eligible cooperative defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “eligible cooperative” means a cooperative organization described in section 1381(a) which is owned more than 50 percent by agricultural producers or by entities owned by agricultural producers. For this purpose an entity owned by an agricultural producer is one that is more than 50 percent owned by agricultural producers.

children.heading: Increase in credit in energy communities
children.label: 7 Increase in credit in energy communities
children.text: In the case of any qualified facility which is located in an energy community (as defined in section 45(b)(11)(B)), for purposes of determining the amount of the credit under subsection (a) with respect to any electricity produced by the taxpayer at such facility during the taxable year, the applicable amount under paragraph (2) of such subsection shall be increased by an amount equal to 10 percent of the amount otherwise in effect under such paragraph.

children.heading: Credit reduced for tax-exempt bonds
children.label: 8 Credit reduced for tax-exempt bonds
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(3) shall apply.

children.heading: Wage requirements
children.label: 9 Wage requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7) shall apply.

children.heading: Apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 10 Apprenticeship requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(8) shall apply.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any qualified facility which satisfies the requirement under subparagraph (B)(i), the amount of the credit determined under subsection (a) shall be increased by an amount equal to 10 percent of the amount so determined (as determined without application of paragraph (7)).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The requirement described in this subclause is satisfied with respect to any qualified facility if the taxpayer certifies to the Secretary (at such time, and in such form and manner, as the Secretary may prescribe) that any steel, iron, or manufactured product which is a component of such facility (upon completion of construction) was produced in the United States (as determined under , Code of Federal Regulations).section 661 of title 49

children.heading: Steel and iron
children.label: ii Steel and iron
children.text: In the case of steel or iron, clause (i) shall be applied in a manner consistent with section 661.5 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: Manufactured product
children.label: iii Manufactured product
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the manufactured products which are components of a qualified facility upon completion of construction shall be deemed to have been produced in the United States if not less than the adjusted percentage (as determined under subparagraph (C)) of the total costs of all such manufactured products of such facility are attributable to manufactured products (including components) which are mined, produced, or manufactured in the United States.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins before , 40 percent,January 1, 2025

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , and before , 45 percent,December 31, 2024January 1, 2026

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , and before , 50 percent, andDecember 31, 2025January 1, 2027

children.label: IV
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , 55 percent.December 31, 2026

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins before , 20 percent,January 1, 2025

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , and before , 27.5 percent,December 31, 2024January 1, 2026

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , and before , 35 percent,December 31, 2025January 1, 2027

children.label: IV
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , and before , 45 percent, andDecember 31, 2026January 1, 2028

children.label: V
children.text: in the case of a facility the construction of which begins after , 55 percent.December 31, 2027

children.label: i
children.text: the value of such credit (determined without regard to this paragraph), multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the applicable percentage.

children.heading: 100 percent applicable percentage for certain qualified facilities
children.label: B 100 percent applicable percentage for certain qualified facilities
i
children.text: which satisfies the requirements under paragraph (11)(B), or

children.label: ii
children.text: with a maximum net output of less than 1 megawatt (as measured in alternating current),

children.label: i
children.text: if construction of such facility began before , 100 percent,January 1, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: if construction of such facility began in calendar year 2024, 90 percent,

children.label: iii
children.text: if construction of such facility began in calendar year 2025, 85 percent, and

children.label: iv
children.text: if construction of such facility began after , 0 percent.December 31, 2025

children.label: I
children.text: the inclusion of steel, iron, or manufactured products which are produced in the United States increases the overall costs of construction of qualified facilities by more than 25 percent, or

children.label: II
children.text: relevant steel, iron, or manufactured products are not produced in the United States in sufficient and reasonably available quantities or of a satisfactory quality.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: ii Applicable percentage
children.text: In any case in which the Secretary provides an exception pursuant to clause (i), the applicable percentage shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities
children.label: 13 Restrictions relating to prohibited foreign entities
i
children.text: a specified foreign entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(B)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: a foreign-influenced entity (as defined in section 7701(a)(51)(D), without regard to clause (i)(II) thereof).

children.heading: Effective control
children.label: B Effective control
children.text: In the case of a taxpayer for which section 7701(a)(51)(D)(i)(II) is determined to apply for any taxable year, no credit shall be determined under subsection (a) for such taxable year if such determination relates to a qualified facility described in subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Denial of credit for wind and solar leasing arrangements
children.label: h Denial of credit for wind and solar leasing arrangements
children.text: No credit shall be determined under this section with respect to any production of electricity during the taxable year with respect to property described in paragraph (1) or (4) of section 25D(d) (as applied by substituting “lessee” for “taxpayer”) if the taxpayer rents or leases such property to a third party during such taxable year.
